label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The user wants to know what the World Bank study in 1974 concluded regarding the benefits of economic growth in developing countries. First, I'll look back at the provided text. The relevant part says, "A major study by the World Bank in 1974 concluded that it is now clear that more than decades of rapid growth in developing countries has been of little benefit to a third of their population." Wait, that wording is a bit tricky. It says "little benefit to a third," which means that a third of the population didn't get much benefit, but what about the rest? The study is pointing out that even with growth, a significant portion (a third) didn't benefit much. The options are A to D. Option A is "benefit to a third of their population." But the text says the growth was "of little benefit to a third of their population." So if it's of little benefit to a third, that implies that only a small portion (a third) got little, but maybe the remaining two-thirds did get some benefit. However, the options don't include that nuance.
